key_insight: The paper shows that agentic AI systems can help formally build and machine-check Lean proofs of Shor-style quantum attacks, including resource estimates for RSA-2048 and P-256.
engineering_relevance: It advances trustworthy quantum-cryptography analysis by turning high-level attack cost reasoning into verified formal artifacts that can support security evaluation and proof-carrying toolchains.
method: Agents search and reuse existing formal developments while writing and repairing Lean proofs.
The formalization covers order finding, reversible quantum circuits, modular arithmetic, and elliptic-curve arithmetic.
Human reviewers validate the scientific claims, and Lean machine-checks the proofs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports formalizations of resource estimates for RSA-2048 and P-256, based on prior results from Quantum 5 and ASIACRYPT 2017, plus added classical-operation estimates.
watch_item: The main limitation is that the results are formal proofs and resource estimates, not practical quantum implementations or demonstrated cryptanalytic speedups on real hardware.
paper_title: Building Shor's Algorithm in Lean: An Agentic Formalization of Quantum Attacks on RSA-2048 and P-256
